L1.area: Comment rendering and content display logic

L2.description: A cross-site scripting (XSS) vulnerability exists where user-controlled metadata fields in submitted comments are rendered directly as HTML without proper sanitization, allowing attackers to inject and execute arbitrary scripts in other users' browsers.

L3.area: Comment card rendering and user-agent metadata display in the main comment widget
L3.description: A cross-site scripting (XSS) vulnerability exists where the user-agent string submitted with comments is parsed and rendered directly into HTML elements without sanitization. An attacker can craft a malicious user-agent value in a comment POST request that, when displayed to other users viewing the comment section, executes arbitrary JavaScript in their browsers.

ground_truth.reason: The comment rendering logic inserts user-controlled user-agent metadata directly into HTML template strings without sanitization, enabling cross-site scripting via crafted POST requests.
